bafta.best_film.notes: Producers credited; team rows with multi-name recipients

bafta.best_director.notes: BAFTA parity rule allows 6 or 7 nominees in some years

bafta.best_leading_actor.notes: BAFTA often has 6 nominees for acting categories

bafta.best_leading_actress.notes: BAFTA often has 6 nominees for acting categories

bafta.best_supporting_actor.notes: BAFTA often has 6 nominees for acting categories

bafta.best_supporting_actress.notes: BAFTA often has 6 nominees for acting categories

bafta.best_animated_film.notes: Re-introduced at 60th BAFTAs (2007). Sometimes 3-4 nominees in lean years

bafta.best_documentary.notes: Reinstated 2012 (65th BAFTAs) after hiatus since 1989. Inaugural slate was 3 nominees; settled to 5 from ceremony 66 onwards. Tolerance widened to 2 as interim fix to cover inaugural-year 3-nominee slate. era_overrides below encodes proper era-specific bounds; wrapper does not yet read it.

era_overrides.reason: Inaugural year of revived category. Per FilmAffinity / Wikipedia, BAFTA awarded only 3 nominations: Senna (winner) and 2 others.

bafta.best_editing.notes: Sometimes editing teams credited; co_recipient split possible

bafta.outstanding_british_film.notes: BAFTA expanded the Outstanding British Film slate to 10 nominees from ceremony 74 (2021) onwards. Earlier ceremonies had 5-6 nominees. Tolerance widened to 5 as interim fix to cover both eras. Future enhancement: the era_overrides array below encodes the proper era-specific bounds; the wrapper does not yet read it.

era_overrides.reason: Pre-expansion era (5-6 nominees). The 73rd BAFTAs (2020) had 6; older ceremonies had 5 or 6. The expansion to 10 took effect at the 74th (2021).

bafta.outstanding_debut.notes: Writer/director/producer team category; recipient_type must be set to a multi-person value to avoid silent splits

bafta.best_childrens_film.notes: New category introduced 2025; initial slates may be smaller

bafta.ee_rising_star.notes: Public vote category; no film_title expected on rows
